text: Annual Leave and Holiday Policy Employees are entitled to 21 days of paid annual leave per calendar year. In addition, employees get 8 sick days and 10 public holidays. Sick leaves extending beyond 3 consecutive days require a medical certificate. Leave requests must be submitted via the HRMS portal at least 2 weeks in advance for planned vacations.
title: Annual Leave and Holiday Policy

text: Work from Home (WFH) Guidelines Our company follows a hybrid work model. Employees are expected to be in the office 3 days a week (Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday). Monday and Friday are flexible Work from Home days. For fully remote roles, employees must ensure a stable internet connection of at least 50 Mbps.
title: Work from Home (WFH) Guidelines

text: Expense Reimbursement Process All employee expenses must be filed via the 'Expensify' app. Meal allowance during client travel is capped at ₹1,500 per day. Hotel bookings must not exceed ₹6,000 per night for Tier-1 cities. Reimbursements are processed on the 15th and 30th of every month. Receipts are mandatory for any expense above ₹500.
title: Expense Reimbursement Process

text: Sales Team Commission Structure 2024 Sales Account Managers earn a base salary plus commission. The commission rate is 5% of the first-year Annual Contract Value (ACV) for a new enterprise client. For contract renewals, the commission is 2% of the renewal value. Commissions are paid out quarterly. The quarterly quota for a Senior Account Manager is ₹1.5 crore.
title: Sales Team Commission Structure 2024

text: New Employee Onboarding Schedule New hires undergo a 3-day onboarding process. Day 1: HR induction and laptop setup (9 AM to 1 PM), followed by a welcome lunch. Day 2: Product architecture overview and shadowing support calls. Day 3: Manager 1-on-1 and goal setting for the first 30 days.

text: Razorpay Pricing and Fee Structure - Internal Analysis Razorpay Domestic Pricing (as of 2024): Standard MDR for credit cards: 2% per transaction. Debit cards: 0.40% for transactions below ₹2,000, 0.75% above ₹2,000. UPI: 0% (zero MDR per NPCI mandate). Net banking: ₹7 flat per transaction. Wallets (Paytm, PhonePe): 1.5% per transaction. EMI on cards: 1.5% per transaction. Enterprise clients with annual volume above ₹500 crore can negotiate rates down to 1.4-1.65% on cards. Razorpay charges no setup fee and no monthly fee. Settlement: T+1 for UPI and T+2 for cards by default. Instant settlement available at 0.25% additional charge. International payments: Razorpay supports 100+ currencies. International MDR: 3% + currency conversion at standard bank rates. Chargeback fee: ₹350 per disputed transaction. Refund: Full refund free, partial refund free. Razorpay Route (split payments/marketplace): 0.25% routing fee. Subscriptions/recurring: No extra charge. Razorpay Capital (lending): Separate product, no impact on MDR.
title: Razorpay Pricing and Fee Structure - Internal Analysis

text: Stripe Pricing and Fee Structure - Internal Analysis Stripe Standard Pricing (as of 2024): All cards (domestic and international): 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction. This is a flat rate with no negotiation for standard accounts. Enterprise pricing available for volume above $1M/month — negotiated rates typically 2.2-2.5% + $0.25. International card surcharge: +1.5% additional for cards issued outside your country. Currency conversion: 1% fee if currency conversion required. Stripe does not support UPI natively — significant disadvantage for India-first businesses. ACH bank transfers (US): 0.8% capped at $5. SEPA Direct Debit (Europe): 0.8% capped at €5. Stripe Billing (subscriptions): Included at no extra cost for standard plans. Stripe Connect (marketplace): 0.25% + standard processing fees. Dispute/chargeback fee: $15 per dispute. Refunds: Processing fees not returned on refunds — this is a key difference from Razorpay. Stripe Radar (fraud): Included in standard pricing. Stripe Terminal (in-person): 2.7% + $0.05 per transaction. Settlement: 2 business days (US), 7 business days for new accounts.
title: Stripe Pricing and Fee Structure - Internal Analysis

text: PayU India Pricing and Fee Structure - Internal Analysis PayU India Pricing (as of 2024): Credit cards: 1.99% per transaction. Debit cards: 0.40% below ₹2,000, 0.75% above ₹2,000 (same as RBI mandate). UPI: 0% (NPCI mandate). Net banking: ₹6 per transaction. EMI: 1.5%. International: 3% + $0.30. PayU enterprise accounts (above ₹200 crore annual volume) can negotiate 1.55-1.80% on cards. PayU is part of Prosus group. Settlement: T+2 standard, T+1 available on request. PayU known for strong presence in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities. PayU LazyPay (BNPL): Available, separate agreement. PayU Payout (bulk disbursement): ₹3-5 per transaction flat. Dispute management portal less sophisticated than Razorpay or Stripe. Customer support: Phone and email, response time 24-48 hours.
title: PayU India Pricing and Fee Structure - Internal Analysis

text: Razorpay vs Stripe - Head to Head Comparison for Enterprise Clients Razorpay vs Stripe - Key Differences for Enterprise Decision Making. Use Razorpay when: 90%+ transactions are domestic India. Need UPI support (0% MDR advantage). Need NACH/e-mandate for subscriptions. Require Indian regulatory compliance out of the box. Need vernacular language support in checkout. Have volume above ₹100 crore for negotiated rates. Use Stripe when: More than 40% revenue is international. Need multi-currency billing (USD, EUR, GBP, etc.). Serving US or European customers primarily. Need advanced developer APIs and webhooks. Building a global SaaS product. Cost comparison example: ₹10 lakh domestic transaction via Razorpay at 1.75% = ₹17,500. Same via Stripe at 2.9% = ₹29,000. Razorpay saves ₹11,500 per ₹10 lakh in domestic volume. For international transactions: Razorpay charges 3%, Stripe charges 2.9% + 1.5% international surcharge = 4.4%. Razorpay is cheaper internationally too. Verdict for pure Indian businesses: Razorpay wins on cost. Verdict for international SaaS: Stripe wins on coverage and features. Hybrid strategy recommendation: Use Razorpay for India billing, Stripe for international billing.
title: Razorpay vs Stripe - Head to Head Comparison for Enterprise Clients

text: CCAvenue Pricing and Overview CCAvenue (Infibeam Avenues) Pricing 2024: Credit cards: 2% per transaction. Debit cards: 1.25% per transaction (higher than competitor mandated rates — legacy issue). Net banking: ₹7 per transaction. UPI: 0% (mandate). International: 3.5% per transaction. CCAvenue has 200+ payment options, one of the widest in India. Strong in government, education, and utility payments. Older technology stack compared to Razorpay. Dashboard and reporting less sophisticated. Settlement: T+3 standard. CCAvenue Xtend (EMI): 1.75%. Enterprise rates negotiable above ₹50 crore annual volume. Key weakness: Debit card MDR is above market rate. Customer support: Average response time 48-72 hours. Best suited for: Government portals, educational institutions, traditional businesses not needing modern API integration.
title: CCAvenue Pricing and Overview

text: International Payment Processing - Deep Dive International Payment Processing Options for Indian Companies 2024. For Indian companies accepting payments from international customers: Razorpay International: Accepts 100+ currencies. Settles in INR. MDR: 3% flat. No international surcharge. Requires FEMA compliance and AD bank tie-up. Good for companies doing below $500K annual international volume. Stripe (with Indian entity): Accepts 135+ currencies. Settles in INR, USD, EUR. MDR: 2.9% + $0.30 + 1.5% international surcharge (total ~4.4% for cross-border). Better developer experience. Required for companies with heavy US/EU customer base. PayPal (for international): MDR: 3.49% + $0.49. High fees but high consumer trust in US. Useful for B2C international. Cashfree International: 3% + $0.30. Emerging option. OPGSP (Overseas Payment Service Provider) model: Indian companies can accept international cards through Razorpay/Stripe acting as OPGSP. Regulatory note: All international payments must be reported to AD Bank within 7 days under FEMA. GST not applicable on international transactions (zero-rated exports). Key decision factor: If more than 30% of your revenue is international, evaluate Stripe. Below 30%, Razorpay international is sufficient and cheaper.
title: International Payment Processing - Deep Dive

text: Payment Processor Reliability and Uptime Comparison 2024 Uptime and Reliability Data for Major Payment Processors in India (2024): Razorpay: 99.97% uptime in 2023. 2 major incidents: Oct 2023 UPI outage (45 mins), Jan 2024 netbanking slowdown (2 hours). Mean time to resolve (MTTR): 38 minutes. Stripe: 99.99% uptime globally. India-specific incidents: March 2024 Indian card authentication delay (30 mins). MTTR: 22 minutes. PayU: 99.93% uptime. Multiple incidents in 2023 including a 4-hour settlement processing delay in Q3. MTTR: 92 minutes — highest among top processors. CCAvenue: 99.88% uptime. Known for periodic slowdowns during peak festival season (Diwali). Razorpay Smart Routing: Automatically retries failed transactions on alternate bank routes — effectively increases success rate by 3-4%. Recommended SLA to include in contracts: Minimum 99.95% uptime guarantee, ₹X penalty per hour of downtime beyond SLA. Current clients with SLA clauses: Meesho, CRED, Groww, Swiggy Instamart.
title: Payment Processor Reliability and Uptime Comparison 2024

text: Payment Processor Settlement Timelines - Client Impact Analysis Settlement Timeline Comparison and Business Impact (2024). Standard settlement timelines: Razorpay: T+1 for UPI, T+2 for cards. Instant settlement (add 0.25% fee). Stripe: T+2 for established accounts, T+7 for new accounts. Payouts on Friday settle Monday. PayU: T+2 standard, T+1 available for premium accounts. CCAvenue: T+3 standard, T+1 available at extra cost. Business impact of settlement delay: A client with ₹5 crore daily GMV at T+2 settlement = ₹10 crore permanently locked in float. At 8% cost of capital, this is ₹80 lakh annual opportunity cost. Instant settlement at 0.25% cost: For ₹5 crore daily volume = ₹1.25 lakh daily charge = ₹4.5 crore annual. Not worth it for most businesses — float opportunity cost is lower. Recommendation: Negotiate T+1 standard settlement (without extra charge) for accounts above ₹100 crore annual volume. Current clients on T+1: CRED, Meesho, PharmEasy, Groww. Clients with settlement complaints: Juspay (T+3 delay in Q1 2024), Dunzo (wants T+1 but on Stripe standard which is T+2).
title: Payment Processor Settlement Timelines - Client Impact Analysis

text: UPI and RuPay - Zero MDR Impact on Business UPI Zero MDR — Business Implications 2024. NPCI Mandate: Since January 2020, UPI transactions carry 0% MDR for merchants. This is a government mandate. No payment processor charges MDR on UPI regardless of volume. UPI market share in India: 80% of all digital transactions by volume (2024). Average UPI transaction size: ₹1,200. Impact on payment processor revenue: Razorpay, PayU, and others earn nothing on UPI transactions. They earn only on cards, netbanking, EMI, and wallets. Strategic implication for clients: If your transaction mix is 70%+ UPI, your blended MDR is very low. Example: Meesho — 75% UPI volume → blended MDR of 0.38% despite 1.75% card rate. Zepto — 80% UPI volume → blended MDR of 0.36%. RuPay cards: Also 0% MDR since 2019. Premium RuPay credit cards: 0% MDR for merchants. CBDC (Digital Rupee): Pilot stage, 0% MDR expected. Implication for processor choice: For high-UPI businesses, processor MDR differences matter less. Focus on reliability, settlement speed, and value-added services instead.
title: UPI and RuPay - Zero MDR Impact on Business

text: Chargeback and Dispute Management - Processor Comparison Chargeback and Dispute Management Comparison 2024. Chargeback rates by industry (India averages): E-commerce: 0.6-0.8%. Subscription SaaS: 0.3-0.5%. Food delivery: 0.2-0.4%. Travel: 1.2-1.8%. Chargeback fee comparison: Razorpay: ₹350 per dispute, regardless of outcome. Stripe: $15 (approx ₹1,250) per dispute, refunded if merchant wins. PayU: ₹250 per dispute. CCAvenue: ₹400 per dispute. Dispute resolution time: Razorpay: Average 15 business days. Stripe: Average 75-90 days (US timeline applies). PayU: Average 21 business days. Razorpay advantage: Disputes resolved under Indian banking rules — faster and more predictable. Stripe advantage: Stripe Radar AI fraud detection reduces disputes by 25% proactively. Current clients with high chargeback rates: Globex Technologies (international cards = high dispute rate), Dunzo (delivery disputes). Recommendation: High-dispute clients should use Razorpay for lower per-dispute cost and faster resolution.
title: Chargeback and Dispute Management - Processor Comparison

text: Internal Sales Playbook - Payment Processor Objection Handling Common Objections and Responses for Payment Processor Comparisons. Objection 1: 'Stripe has better developer experience.' Response: Razorpay APIs are equally well-documented and specifically optimized for Indian payment rails. Razorpay has SDKs for React, Android, iOS, Flutter. Razorpay Checkout is a single line of code. Developer NPS for Razorpay in India is 68 vs Stripe's global NPS of 72 — nearly equivalent. Objection 2: 'Stripe supports more countries.' Response: True for pure international play. But Razorpay covers 100+ currencies for acceptance. If your business is India-first with some international, Razorpay is superior. Only choose Stripe if 40%+ revenue is from outside India. Objection 3: 'Our current processor is good enough.' Response: Calculate their current blended MDR and compare to what Razorpay can offer. For a ₹100 crore annual volume business, a 0.3% MDR reduction saves ₹30 lakh per year. Objection 4: 'Switching payment processors is too risky.' Response: Razorpay migration team handles full switchover. Zero downtime migration plan. Parallel run for 2 weeks to validate. Reference: Dunzo migrated from PayU to Razorpay in 3 weeks with zero downtime in 2022. Objection 5: 'We are already on Stripe and everything works.' Response: Fair. For
title: Internal Sales Playbook - Payment Processor Objection Handling

text: switchover. Zero downtime migration plan. Parallel run for 2 weeks to validate. Reference: Dunzo migrated from PayU to Razorpay in 3 weeks with zero downtime in 2022. Objection 5: 'We are already on Stripe and everything works.' Response: Fair. For international SaaS, Stripe is the right choice. Only push Razorpay if they have significant domestic volume being processed at 2.9%.
title: Internal Sales Playbook - Payment Processor Objection Handling

text: BNPL and Credit Line Products - Processor Comparison Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) Options through Payment Processors 2024. Razorpay BNPL Partners: Simpl, ZestMoney, LazyPay, Flexmoney. MDR on BNPL: 2.0-2.5% merchant fee, higher than standard card MDR. Razorpay manages all BNPL partners through single integration. Stripe: No native BNPL in India. Klarna and Afterpay not available in Indian market. Merchants must integrate BNPL separately outside Stripe. PayU LazyPay: PayU's own BNPL product. 2.5% merchant fee. 3.5 crore active users. Strong in Tier-2 cities. Conversion impact of BNPL: Adding BNPL typically increases checkout conversion by 12-18% for average ticket sizes above ₹1,500. Current clients using BNPL: Nykaa Fashion (via Razorpay — all major BNPL partners), Meesho (Simpl integration via Razorpay). Clients who should consider BNPL: PharmEasy (average ₹680 ticket — borderline), UrbanCompany (₹1,100 average — strong candidate). BNPL regulatory risk: RBI has tightened BNPL regulations in 2023. Ensure clients are aware that BNPL lenders must be RBI-registered NBFCs.
title: BNPL and Credit Line Products - Processor Comparison

text: GST and Tax Compliance on Payment Processing Fees GST on Payment Processing Fees 2024. Payment gateway service fees are subject to 18% GST in India. This is charged on top of the MDR. Example: Razorpay charges 1.75% MDR + 18% GST on that fee. Effective cost to merchant: 1.75% * 1.18 = 2.065% of transaction value. Stripe charges 2.9% MDR + 18% GST. Effective cost: 2.9% * 1.18 = 3.422%. GST credit: If the merchant is GST-registered (B2B), they can claim input tax credit (ITC) on the GST paid to the payment processor. This effectively reduces the net cost. For a GST-registered enterprise client: Net effective MDR after ITC = base MDR only (GST neutralized). For B2C merchants (not GST registered): Full 2.065% or 3.422% is the actual cost. Implication: For enterprise clients (all B2B, GST-registered), the GST component of MDR is a pass-through. The actual comparison should be on base MDR only: Razorpay 1.75% vs Stripe 2.9% — net of GST. Compliance: Payment processors must issue GST invoices monthly. All our processors are compliant.
title: GST and Tax Compliance on Payment Processing Fees

text: Payment Processor Integration and Technical Evaluation Technical Integration Comparison 2024. API Quality: Razorpay: REST API, well-documented, India-specific (UPI, NACH, QR). Stripe: REST API, industry gold standard, better webhook reliability, superior testing tools (Stripe CLI). PayU: REST API, older design, less consistent error responses. Integration time estimate: Razorpay basic checkout: 1-2 days. Razorpay full integration (subscriptions, payouts, webhooks): 1-2 weeks. Stripe basic: 2-3 days (more complex initial setup). Stripe full: 2-3 weeks. Mobile SDK quality: Razorpay Android/iOS SDK — excellent, 4.6/5 Play Store. Stripe Mobile SDK — excellent, 4.7/5 Play Store. Webhook reliability: Stripe webhooks: 99.98% delivery, automatic retry, full event log. Razorpay webhooks: 99.90% delivery, manual retry required on failure. This gap matters for subscription billing and payout automation. PCI DSS Compliance: All major processors (Razorpay, Stripe, PayU) are PCI DSS Level 1 compliant. Merchants using hosted checkout are automatically compliant. Testing environment: Stripe test mode is superior — all card scenarios, simulated failures, full parity with production. Razorpay sandbox is adequate but has gaps (NACH testing limited).
title: Payment Processor Integration and Technical Evaluation

text: Support Ticket #4821 - Zepto UPI Failure During Sale Ticket #4821. Client: Zepto. Priority: P1. Date: March 15, 2024. Issue: UPI transactions failing at 22% rate between 8 PM - 10 PM during their weekend sale event. Normal failure rate is 2%. Impact: Estimated ₹4.2 crore in lost GMV. Resolution: Root cause identified as NPCI throttling during peak hour. Razorpay smart routing was not configured for Zepto's account. Fix: Enabled multi-bank UPI routing (HDFC + Kotak + ICICI fallback). Failure rate dropped to 1.8% post-fix. Time to resolve: 3.5 hours. Compensation offered: ₹50,000 credit on next invoice. Follow-up: Capacity planning meeting scheduled before next sale event.
title: Support Ticket #4821 - Zepto UPI Failure During Sale

text: Support Ticket #3917 - Juspay Settlement Delay Ticket #3917. Client: Juspay India. Priority: P2. Date: February 3, 2024. Issue: Settlement delay of 3 additional business days beyond contracted T+2. Affected ₹28 crore in settlements. Root cause: Banking partner (Yes Bank) processing queue backlog during their internal system migration. Impact: Juspay had to arrange bridge financing, incurring ₹18 lakh in interest costs. Resolution: Settlement processed manually via alternate banking partner. Issue resolved in 5 days total. Compensation: Offered ₹12 lakh credit — Juspay rejected, demanding full ₹18 lakh reimbursement. Status: Escalated to VP Sales. Unresolved. This is the primary reason for Juspay's low health score of 68/100. Action required: Legal to review contract SLA clause. Finance to assess compensation exposure.
title: Support Ticket #3917 - Juspay Settlement Delay

text: Support Ticket #5102 - Groww NACH Rejection Rate Ticket #5102. Client: Groww. Priority: P2. Date: April 2, 2024. Issue: NACH (National Automated Clearing House) mandate rejections at 6% vs industry average of 3.5%. Affects SIP (Systematic Investment Plan) auto-debit. Impact: Each rejected mandate = failed SIP = user drops SIP = Groww loses recurring investment user. At 95 lakh transactions/month, 6% rejection = 5.7 lakh failed SIPs monthly. Customer impact is significant. Root cause analysis: Banks rejecting mandates due to account name mismatch (full name vs short name in NACH database). Razorpay NACH does not do pre-validation of account holder name before mandate creation. Fix proposed: Add account name fuzzy-matching pre-check before NACH registration. Implementation timeline: 6 weeks (engineering). Interim workaround: Add retry logic with alternative account details prompt. Groww engineering team engaged directly. Weekly sync scheduled.
title: Support Ticket #5102 - Groww NACH Rejection Rate

text: Support Ticket #4455 - Dunzo MDR Cost Complaint Ticket #4455. Client: Dunzo Daily. Priority: P3 (commercial). Date: March 8, 2024. Issue: Not a technical issue. Commercial complaint — Dunzo's Finance team escalated that their Stripe MDR at 2.9% is significantly higher than what competitors are paying on Razorpay. They have received a proposal from Razorpay offering 1.85% for their volume. Financial impact: Dunzo processes ₹420 crore annually. At Stripe's 2.9% = ₹12.18 crore annual processing cost. At Razorpay's 1.85% = ₹7.77 crore. Difference = ₹4.41 crore per year. Account Manager note: Dunzo is on Stripe due to a 2021 decision by their previous CTO who preferred Stripe's developer experience. Current tech team does not have strong preference. This is a commercial issue. Action: Rahul Nair to present retention proposal by March 25. Status: Proposal submitted, awaiting Dunzo decision. Renewal due September 2024 — this is critical.
title: Support Ticket #4455 - Dunzo MDR Cost Complaint

text: Support Ticket #5298 - BrowserStack Indian Card Failures Ticket #5298. Client: BrowserStack. Priority: P2. Date: April 18, 2024. Issue: 23% of Indian credit card transactions failing on Stripe due to RBI 2FA (two-factor authentication) requirement. Stripe's 3DS implementation has compatibility issues with some Indian bank OTP flows. Impact: BrowserStack estimates ₹8 crore in lost annual India revenue from declined cards. Root cause: Stripe processes Indian cards through international routing which hits additional authentication layers. Resolution path: BrowserStack to add Razorpay for India-origin customers only. Stripe continues for international. This is the split-processor strategy. Implementation plan: BrowserStack dev team to implement geo-based processor routing (India IP → Razorpay, Others → Stripe). Timeline: 4 weeks. Expected outcome: Indian card success rate improves from 77% to 96%. Estimated revenue recovery: ₹6-7 crore annually.
title: Support Ticket #5298 - BrowserStack Indian Card Failures
